repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §1 Q1_MinimalBasis/Q1_StaggeredMinimalBasis give binary FWL contamination decompositions, not a continuous-dose dCDH-vs-TWFE ACR Riesz row

published_prior_art.cite: CallawayGoodmanBaconSantAnna2024 and deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2023SeveralTreatments give continuous-TWFE / multi-treatment contamination warnings, but not this dCDH-TWFE no-remainder row

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_bjs_efficient_event_study_v1: rank-zero IF certificate for binary staggered event-study estimators; panel_sdid_mc_id_v1: SCM/ASCM/SDiD/MC span lattice, not DHS/dCDH/TWFE continuous-dose ACR

published_prior_art.cite: DHaultfoeuilleHoderleinSasaki2023, deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuillePasquierSowVazquezBare2025, and CallawayGoodmanBaconSantAnna2024 identify/decompose separate continuous-treatment DiD objects; none gives a three-method iff equality frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_bjs_efficient_event_study_v1 asks IF equality for binary staggered estimators, not common-submodel score equality for DHS/dCDH/TWFE continuous-dose ACR

published_prior_art.cite: BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024 gives robust/efficient binary event-study imputation benchmarks; no verified paper puts DHS, dCDH, and continuous-dose TWFE in one tangent-space score frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §1 panel entries prove binary FWL/TWFE contamination and sign results, not open-set DHS/dCDH/TWFE continuous-dose strict separation

published_prior_art.cite: GoodmanBacon2021, SunAbraham2021, deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2020, deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2023SeveralTreatments, and CallawayGoodmanBaconSantAnna2024EventStudies give adjacent contamination/aggregation results, not this three-estimator open-set frontier

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Condition (i) is pointwise equality at the realized law, while (ii) is operator rank-zero on the whole active residual subspace; a nonzero row can vanish at the realized \bar A, so the statement needs a universal local-law/perturbation quantifier or (ii) should be weakened to K^{func}_{h,\omega}(d)\bar A_{h,\omega}(d)=0.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: K^{score}=0 is defined only through vectorized observable-cell projection differences; equality of full projected influence scores in \mathcal T_{h,\omega} needs an explicit no-orthogonal-score-component assumption.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: \kappa^{func}_{h,\omega}(d)>0 says the operator is nonzero, not that the realized gap is nonzero; strict separation needs K^{func}_{h,\omega}(d)\bar A_{h,\omega}(d)\ne0 or an active-component condition.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Rewrite the frontier as two separate statements: realized-law equality iff K^{func}\bar A=0, and uniform local-frontier equality over all admissible perturbations iff \kappa^{func}=0.
Add a short lemma/corollary that separates operator rank-zero from realized-gap-zero with a one-dimensional null-vector example, so the diagnostic interpretation is precise.

For Conjecture 2, explicitly decompose each score difference into observable-cell and orthogonal-tangent components, then make K^{score}=0 control both or state the extra zero-orthogonal-component assumption.

recommended_next_step: Revise the kernel quantifiers and realized-gap conditions before sending this to Stage 0.
